justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Domain-architecture conflict explicitly identified — the tension between Bird's absolute 30s invariant and distributed system realities is the central escalation. (2) Formal escalation with specific questions — the escalation raises the impossibility of guaranteeing the invariant absolutely and presents three options for Bird to decide, including scale unknowns in low_confidence_areas (concurrent couriers, geographic distribution). (3) Multiple architectural options presented with trade-offs — PostgreSQL-only, PostgreSQL+materialized view, TimescaleDB, and the recommended Redis+PostgreSQL dual-write are all compared. (4) trade_offs includes sacrifices for the recommended option (Redis as infra dependency, ephemeral data, dual-write consistency concerns). (5) risks includes PostgreSQL throughput concerns (async writes, partitioning needs noted in low_confidence_areas). (6) MJ does not commit to a final design without missing information — explicitly flags scale requirements as unknown and Bird's acceptance of softened invariant as unresolved. (7) confidence.level is 55, well below the 70 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough escalation that reframes the problem honestly — Bird's absolute invariant is physically impossible in distributed systems, and MJ names this directly rather than hand-waving
Confidence level of 55 appropriately reflects the significant unknowns (fleet size, Bird's acceptance of softened invariant, PostgreSQL capacity)
Trade-offs section is well-structured with explicit sacrifices and alternatives_considered for each decision
Defense-in-depth approach (TTL + server check + client timer) shows sophisticated understanding of the failure modes
Risks section covers Redis failure, dual-write inconsistency, clock skew, WebSocket stalls, and high courier density — comprehensive

The escalation focuses more on the distributed systems impossibility than on explicitly requesting fleet size and update frequency numbers — these appear in low_confidence_areas but could be more prominent as blocking questions

MJ goes quite deep into implementation details (file paths, Redis commands, phased rollout) for an architecture that acknowledges critical unknowns — this level of detail may create false confidence in a design that could change based on escalation outcomes
